etymology.description: semantic 糸 (silk; thread) + phonetic 合 (join; unite) -> rich, abundant -> to give
etymology.interpretation: The character 給 combines the semantic component 糸 (silk; thread), which conveys abundance due to the value of silk, with the phonetic 合 (join; unite). While 合 primarily contributes the sound, the character has evolved to mean 'rich' or 'abundant', later extending to 'provide' or 'give'. The connection from 'abundance' to 'give' arises from the notion of sharing wealth or resources.
etymology.simplification: The traditional form 給 was simplified by changing 糸 (silk; thread) to 纟 to reduce the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 她给了我一本书(她給了我一本書)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěi le wǒ yī běn shū.

examples.chinese: 请把那个杯子给我(請把那個杯子給我)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ nà ge bēizi gěi wǒ.

examples.chinese: 我会为你提供帮助(我會為你提供幫助)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ huì wèi nǐ tígōng bāngzhù.
examples.english: I will provide help for you.
